dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses context-dependent direction with MA crossover logic. Entry uses sma_5 (short-term) and sma_20 (long-term) as representative parameters within the specified ranges (short: 3-21, long: 10-80). Locked-limit day condition cannot be enforced at rule level. Always-in-market constraint is implicit in bidirectional entry/exit structure. Exit conditions are exact reversals of entry conditions per signal specification.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses context-dependent direction with MA crossover logic. Entry uses sma_5 (short-term) and sma_20 (long-term) as representative parameters within the specified ranges (short: 3-21, long: 10-80). Locked-limit day condition cannot be enforced at rule level. Always-in-market constraint is implicit in bidirectional entry/exit structure. Exit conditions are exact reversals of entry conditions per signal specification.

source_rule_text: Buy when the short-term moving average crosses above the long-term moving average; sell short when the short-term moving average crosses below the long-term moving average.
